programmes.show_description: After escaping and being recaptured by Draconians, the Doctor convinces the Draconian prince of the Ogron involvement just as the true manipulator is revealed.
The Master captures the Doctor and Jo and takes them to the Ogron planet to show off his alliance with the Daleks, who plan to exploit the coming interplanetary war.
The Doctor and Jo escape the Master and the Daleks, warn both Earth and Draconia of the real threat, and set off in pursuit of the Daleks to stop their larger invasion plan.

programmes.show_name: Doctor Who: Planet of the Daleks
programmes.show_description: After the Doctor collapses from injuries sustained on Spiridon, Jo seeks help from a group of Thals who reveal the Daleks are secretly forming an invasion force on the planet.

programmes.show_name: Doctor Who: Planet of the Daleks
programmes.show_description: The Doctor, revived by the Thals, discovers that the Daleks are experimenting with invisibility and preparing to unleash a deadly bacterial weapon.

programmes.show_name: Doctor Who: Planet of the Daleks
programmes.show_description: Jo evades capture while the Doctor and the Thals infiltrate a Dalek base, learning of a vast Dalek army hidden in suspended animation.
After escaping and being recaptured by Draconians, the Doctor convinces the Draconian prince of the Ogron involvement just as the true manipulator is revealed.

Shopping from Home.
Paramedics help a patient who fears he's having his third heart attack, and an elderly woman who had a fall at home and may have been lying on the floor all night.
Paramedics need to get a sick baby to hospital. They also treat a man who suffers from epilepsy and has had a suspected stroke, and an elderly lady who needs to take her medication.
Adam and Jenine try to help a woman who's fallen from a first-floor window, while Laura and Natalie deal with a former paramedic who appears to have had a stroke.

Passionfruit: The final series of the sitcom about odd couple Daphne and Sam. Daphne finds it hard to come to terms with Sam's growing friendship with Rhonda.
On the Radio: Series four of the sitcom about flat-sharing in-laws. Daphne confronts Sam's new girlfriend, while Angie gets a screen test for a TV commercial.
Omelettes and Optimism: British domestic sitcom. Sam and Daphne's newly discovered friendship creates a problem for Sam and Rhonda.
Golf: Domestic sitcom. Sam has arranged a surprise for Daphne, but when she tells him that she is not free, he is disappointed and decides to find out why.
The Driving Test: British domestic sitcom. While Angie prepares for her test, Daphne is busy with her own project.

Gorey: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers evidence of the German occupation of the Jersey village of Gorey and the role it played in defending the UK.
Shopping from Home.
North Of The Wall: A complex of stone buildings near Hadrian's Wall that were built to keep Northern hoards at bay! Also, a top secret military facility in the American Midwest.
Blast Zone: Within a dense Polish woodland sits a forgotten complex with an explosive history. Also, grand coastal defences in Ghana reveal a tortured past.
Forbidden Island: Examining a ghost island once belonging to a secretive country on the fringes of Europe. Also, a haunting site in the UK with evidence of strange burial practices.

Cheltenham: Final episode of the domestic sitcom. Sam's visit to Cheltenham for lunch with Nanny Parker is a big hit, but things start to go downhill on the way back to London.
A Real Partnership: With a financial crisis looming just as MPs are due for a pay rise, Humphrey needs all his flair to dress up his claim and push it through. Smart political comedy.
A Victory for Democracy: Classic political sitcom. After a series of difficult encounters, Jim Hacker begins to wonder whether the government runs the Foreign Office, or vice versa.
The Bishop's Gambit: Classic comedy. Jim must recommend the appointment of bishops to the Queen, but is not keen on the two offered by the Church. Humphrey prefers a third candidate.
One of Us: It's Sir Humphrey in trouble for a change when it's revealed he wrongly cleared an MI5 agent accused of spying. Naturally, Jim Hacker is very sympathetic. Vintage laughs.
